title: Getting Started with Scratch
description: Introduction to Scratch: the interface, blocks, and your first script.

steps.title: What is Scratch?
steps.content:
Welcome to Scratch

Scratch is a visual programming language. Instead of typing code, you snap coloured blocks together like puzzle pieces.

Each block is a single instruction. Connecting them in order creates a script — a sequence of steps the computer follows.

Example script

when green flag clicked
move (10) steps
play sound [Meow v] until done

Click Next Step to find out what the different block colours mean.

steps.title: Block Categories
steps.content:
Block categories

Every block belongs to a category. The colour tells you what kind of instruction it is.

Motion — blue

move (10) steps
turn right (15) degrees
go to x: (0) y: (0)

Looks — purple

say [Hello!] for (2) seconds
switch costume to [costume1 v]

Events — yellow (hat blocks)

when green flag clicked
when [space v] key pressed

Hat blocks are rounded at the top — they always go at the start of a script and tell Scratch when to run.

steps.title: Scripts Run in Order
steps.content:
Scripts run top to bottom

When a script starts, Scratch runs each block in order, from top to bottom. The script below makes a sprite move, then say something, then move again.

A sequence

when green flag clicked
move (50) steps
say [I moved!] for (1) seconds
move (50) steps
say [I moved again!] for (1) seconds

Try to predict what a script will do by reading it from top to bottom before you run it.

steps.title: Your First Task
steps.css: .y7-scratch-wrap { margin: 0.75rem 0; border: 1px solid #e2e8f0; border-radius: 0.5rem; overflow: hidden; } .y7-scratch-wrap.y7-fullscreen { position: fixed !important; inset: 0 !important; z-index: 9999 !important; margin: 0 !important; border-radius: 0 !important; border: none !important; background: #111827; } .y7-scratch-frame { display: block; border: none; transform-origin: top left; } .y7-check-bar { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.75rem; padding: 0.6rem 0.75rem; background: #f8fafc; border-top: 1px solid #e2e8f0; } .y7-check-result { font-size: 0.85rem; flex: 1; } .y7-fs-btn { margin-left: auto; background: none; border: 1px solid #cbd5e1; border-radius: 0.3rem; cursor: pointer; padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 1rem; color: #64748b; line-height: 1; } .y7-fs-btn:hover { background: #f1f5f9; color: #374151; }
steps.content:
Your first Scratch task

Use the editor below to build this script. Find Events for the hat block and Motion for the move block, then snap them together.

Build this

when green flag clicked
move (10) steps
steps.js: (function(){var NW=1100,NH=650;function scaleFrame(){var frame=document.getElementById('y7-scratch-frame');if(!frame)return;var wrap=frame.parentElement;var bar=wrap.querySelector('.y7-check-bar');var barH=bar?bar.offsetHeight:44;var isFs=wrap.classList.contains('y7-fullscreen');var availW=isFs?window.innerWidth:wrap.clientWidth;var scale=isFs?Math.min(availW/NW,(window.innerHeight-barH)/NH):availW/NW;frame.style.width=NW+'px';frame.style.height=NH+'px';frame.style.transform='scale('+scale+')';frame.style.marginBottom=Math.round(NH*(scale-1))+'px';frame.style.marginLeft=isFs?Math.max(0,Math.round((availW-NW*scale)/2))+'px':'0';}function toggleFs(){var frame=document.getElementById('y7-scratch-frame');var wrap=frame.parentElement;var btn=document.getElementById('y7-fs-btn');var isFs=wrap.classList.toggle('y7-fullscreen');btn.textContent=isFs?'✕':'⛶';btn.title=isFs?'Exit full screen (Esc)':'Full screen';if(isFs){window._y7ScrollY=window.scrollY;document.body.style.overflow='hidden';}else{document.body.style.overflow='';window.scrollTo(0,window._y7ScrollY||0);}scaleFrame();}scaleFrame();if(window._y7Resize)window.removeEventListener('resize',window._y7Resize);window._y7Resize=scaleFrame;window.addEventListener('resize',window._y7Resize);if(window._y7Key)document.removeEventListener('keydown',window._y7Key);window._y7Key=function(e){if(e.key==='Escape'){var frame=document.getElementById('y7-scratch-frame');if(frame&&frame.parentElement.classList.contains('y7-fullscreen'))toggleFs();}};document.addEventListener('keydown',window._y7Key);document.getElementById('y7-fs-btn').onclick=toggleFs;document.getElementById('y7-check-btn').onclick=function(){var frame=document.getElementById('y7-scratch-frame');var result=document.getElementById('y7-check-result');try{var vm=frame.contentWindow.vm;if(!vm){result.style.color='#fca5a5';result.textContent='Editor still loading — wait a moment and try again.';return;}var targets=vm.runtime.targets;var found=false;for(var t=0;t<targets.length&&!found;t++){var blocks=targets[t].blocks._blocks;for(var id in blocks){var b=blocks[id];if(b.opcode==='event_whenflagclicked'&&b.next){var nb=blocks[b.next];if(nb&&nb.opcode==='motion_movesteps'){found=true;break;}}}}if(found){result.style.color='#86efac';result.textContent='✓ Correct! Green flag connected to move steps.';__markStepComplete();}else{result.style.color='#fca5a5';result.textContent='Not quite — connect the green flag block directly to a move steps block.';}}catch(e){result.style.color='#fca5a5';result.textContent='Could not check: '+e.message;console.error(e);}};})();

quizQuestions.q: What shape is a hat block?
What colour are Motion blocks in Scratch?
What does the green flag block do?
options: Starts the script when the green flag is clicked

quizQuestions.q: In what order does Scratch run the blocks in a script?
Which block would you use to make a sprite move across the stage?
What file extension does a Scratch project use when saved to your computer?
What colour are Events blocks (like 'when green flag clicked')?
How many blocks does the simplest working script need?
options: At least 2 — a hat block and one action block
